topic: Magistral Review 2026: Mistral Open-Weight Reasoning Model That Beats DeepSeek R1

key_points: Official announcement — two variants: Magistral Small (24B, open-source Apache 2.0) and Magistral Medium (enterprise API)
RLVR training on top of Mistral Medium 3 with no distillation from other reasoning models
Magistral Medium: 73.6% AIME-24, 90% majority-vote @64; Magistral Small: 70.7%, 83.3%
Multilingual chain-of-thought across English, French, Spanish, German, Italian, Arabic, Russian, Simplified Chinese
128k context window; performance degrades past 40k
Transparent, auditable reasoning chain vs black-box competitors
Fits on a single RTX 4090 or 32 GB MacBook when quantised
Positioned for law, finance, healthcare, logistics, and software engineering
Flash Answers mode in Le Chat delivers up to 10× faster throughput than most rivals
Known issue: can enter infinite thinking loops on edge cases
Output speed ~38 tokens/sec — slower than lightweight models
Stronger than DeepSeek R1 Zero on AIME-24 majority voting (90% vs comparable)
Proprietary models (Claude Opus 4.6, GPT-5.4) still outperform on hardest benchmarks
Open-source availability enables local fine-tuning and custom deployment
DeepSeek R1 uses 671B parameters vs Magistral Medium (closed-size but MoE-based)
Magistral Medium AIME-24: 73.6% vs DeepSeek R1 comparable; Magistral wins at efficiency per parameter
DeepSeek R1 leads on HumanEval (73.2% pass@1) and SWE-Verified (49.2%)
Magistral scores 59.4% on LiveCodeBench v5 — competitive but not the top coding model
Magistral Small 1.2 and Medium 1.2 released September 2025 (incremental improvements)
Mistral Small 4 (March 2026) merged Magistral + Pixtral + Devstral into one unified model
Devstral 2 positions as a separate coding specialist built on Magistral's reasoning backbone
Enterprise teams prefer Medium via API; hobbyists/researchers prefer Small via Ollama or HuggingFace

stats.fact: Magistral Medium scores 73.6% on AIME-2024 (pass@1) — a 50% improvement over the base Mistral Medium 3 checkpoint
Magistral Medium reaches 90% on AIME-2024 with majority voting @64, matching DeepSeek-R1-Zero performance
Magistral Small (24B parameters) scores 70.7% AIME-24 pass@1 and 83.3% with majority voting
SFT + RL training for Magistral Small yielded >5 point gain over SFT or RL alone across multiple benchmarks
Magistral Medium API pricing: $2.00 per million input tokens — 37% cheaper on output than OpenAI o3
Magistral Small can be quantized and run locally on a single RTX 4090 or a 32 GB RAM MacBook
Multilingual performance is 4.3–9.9% lower than English (1–3 AIME questions), attributed to constrained reasoning language
Output speed runs at approximately 38 tokens/second — slower than lighter models but fast for reasoning workloads
Flash Answers in Le Chat delivers up to 10× faster token throughput compared to most reasoning model competitors
LiveCodeBench v5: Magistral scores 59.4%, vs DeepSeek R1's stronger HumanEval performance (73.2%)

angles: Transparency & auditability: Magistral shows its reasoning chain step-by-step — crucial for regulated industries (finance, legal, healthcare)
Open-weight advantage: run Magistral Small locally for free with full data privacy — no API calls, no per-token fees
Beats DeepSeek R1 Zero at reasoning efficiency: fewer parameters, competitive or better AIME scores
EU-built alternative: Mistral is a French company — GDPR-native, EU AI Act compliance baked-in, no Chinese data concerns
Mistral Small 4 convergence: Magistral reasoning merged into unified model in March 2026 — what this means for long-term planning
Enterprise cost comparison: at $2/M input tokens, Magistral Medium undercuts GPT-4o ($5/M) and Claude Sonnet ($3/M input) while matching reasoning quality
keywords: mistral open source reasoning model beats deepseek
